creator: aehlig@linta.de
text:
Hi Rainer,

thanks for looking at my PR. The I reason, I have not used 'make makepatch' is that it does not interact well with the changes introduced by the 'post-patch' target; doing it naively, I get, for example, the absolute path to my python interpreter in the refreshed patch for src/main/java/com/google/devtools/build/lib/bazel/rules/python/BazelPythonSemantics.java instead of the placeholder string '%%PYTHON_CMD%%'. Do you know I way of refreshing the patches that is less error-prone than 'make clean patch && make makepatch' and manually undoing the algorithmic changes in the generated patches? I would really to have a safe way to (re)generate the patches, as it would make maintaining the port a lot easier.

Thanks,
Klaus
